pronouns.grammar:
NOMINATIV: 'ich' is the subject (the one doing the action).
Personal pronouns (subject form):
• ich (I), du (you), er (he), sie (she), es (it)
• wir (we), ihr (you all), sie (they), Sie (formal you)

pronouns.hint: Female version uses '-in' suffix: Arzt → Ärztin

pronouns.grammar:
DATIV: 'helfen' takes a Dativ object.
• ich → mir, du → dir, er → ihm, sie → ihr
• wir → uns, ihr → euch, sie/Sie → ihnen/Ihnen

Memorize: helfen + Dativ (always!)

AKKUSATIV: 'lieben' takes an Akkusativ object.
• ich → mich, du → dich, er → ihn, sie → sie, es → es
• wir → uns, ihr → euch, sie/Sie → sie/Sie

pronouns.hint: Verb 'helfen' goes to the end after a modal verb

pronouns.grammar:
Possessive pronouns agree with the noun's gender:
• mein Buch (das, neuter), meine Tasche (die, fem), mein Stift (der, masc)
Master table:
• mein/meine/mein (my)
• dein/deine/dein (your)
• sein/seine/sein (his/its)
• ihr/ihre/ihr (her/their)
• unser/unsere/unser (our)

pronouns.hint: 'sie' (her) — Akkusativ same as Nominativ for feminine
'uns' = Dativ (recipient) — Geld is the Akkusativ thing given
'sein' = his (for masculine/neuter possessor of a neuter noun)

pronouns.grammar:
Verb 'geben' has TWO objects:
• indirect (recipient) → DATIV: ihr (her)
• direct (thing given) → AKKUSATIV: das Geschenk

Pattern: Subject + verb + Dativ-person + Akkusativ-thing

pronouns.english: He gave us his phone number
pronouns.hint: Perfekt: haben + Partizip II at the end
Perfekt: haben + Partizip II 'erklärt' at the end

pronouns.grammar:
REFLEXIVE 'sich treffen mit' (to meet with).
'mit' takes DATIV → 'mit ihnen' (with them).
'uns' is the reflexive pronoun ('ourselves').

pronouns.english: I already told her about it
pronouns.hint: Order of pronoun objects: Akkusativ (es) BEFORE Dativ (ihr) when both are pronouns

pronouns.grammar:
REFLEXIVE imperative: 'setz DICH' (sit yourself).
'neben' is a Wechselpräposition — with motion (sitting down) → AKKUSATIV → 'mich'.

pronouns.hint: 'mit' → Dativ → 'ihm'. 'zu + infinitive' at the end of the noun phrase.

pronouns.english: Whoever helps me, I thank them
pronouns.grammar:
RELATIVE PRONOUNS in subordinate clauses:
• 'Wer' (whoever) — Nominativ
• 'dem' — Dativ (because 'danken' takes Dativ)

In the main clause, 'ich' is moved AFTER the verb because the subordinate clause comes first (V2 rule).

pronouns.english: Those who help us, we appreciate
pronouns.hint: 'Diejenigen, die …' = demonstrative 'those who'. Relative clause verb at end.

pronouns.english: I remember you, him, and everyone else
pronouns.grammar:
REFLEXIVE + preposition: 'sich erinnern AN + AKKUSATIV'.
Notice each item gets its own 'an':
• an dich (you), an ihn (him), an alle anderen (all others)

pronouns.english: What you tell me, I can hardly believe
pronouns.hint: 'Was' as relative pronoun = 'that which / what'. Subordinate verb at end, then V2 in main.
'Sie' (capitalized) = formal you OR 'sie' (lowercase) = they; verb 'sind' fits both

pronouns.grammar:
RECIPROCAL pronoun 'uns' (each other):
• Wir sehen uns = we see each other
• Ihr trefft euch = you all meet each other
• Sie helfen sich = they help each other

pronouns.english: My brother gives her a gift

pronouns.grammar:
'warten AUF + AKKUSATIV' (verb + fixed preposition).
Learn whole units: warten auf, denken an, sich freuen auf …

pronouns.english: Without us there is no game
pronouns.hint: 'ohne' + Akkusativ → 'uns' (same form as Nom for plural)

pronouns.english: My friend always helps you all

pronouns.grammar:
'gehören' + DATIV (always!):
• Das Auto gehört mir/dir/ihm/ihr/uns/euch/ihnen.
Note: NOT 'für mich' — gehören doesn't use 'für'.

pronouns.english: When he is tired, she helps him with cleaning up
pronouns.hint: Wenn-clause + main clause V2. 'beim' = bei + dem (Dativ).

pronouns.english: We met her yesterday in the park
pronouns.hint: Perfekt with Akkusativ pronoun 'sie' (her). Word order: TeKaMoLo.

pronouns.english: Have you already washed yourself today?
pronouns.grammar:
REFLEXIVE Perfekt:
• haben + reflexive pronoun (mich/dich/sich/…) + Partizip II
• 'sich waschen' = to wash oneself

pronouns.english: I am looking forward to our trip on the weekend
pronouns.grammar:
'sich freuen AUF + AKKUSATIV' (looking forward to).
Reflexive verb — pronoun 'mich' is mandatory.
'unseren Ausflug' = our trip (Akk masc.)

pronouns.english: She invited us all to her birthday party
Do you remember me from the last trip?
Without her help I would never have managed it
pronouns.grammar:
KONJUNKTIV II (hypothetical past):
• 'hätte' + Partizip II at the end
• Expresses 'would have' (counterfactual)

pronouns.english: My father gave him his old computer
You have to decide whether you come with us
pronouns.grammar:
'sich entscheiden' = REFLEXIVE.
'ob'-clause (whether) = subordinate → verb to end.
'mit' + Dativ → 'mit uns'

pronouns.english: She takes care of her sick mother every day
pronouns.grammar:
'sich kümmern UM + Akk' (to take care of).
Note the reflexive pronoun 'sich' for er/sie/es/Sie.

pronouns.english: Those who helped them, we will never forget
pronouns.grammar:
Relative clause 'die ihnen geholfen haben':
• 'die' (Nom. plural) — refers to 'Diejenigen'
• 'ihnen' (Dat. plural — helfen takes Dativ)
• Main clause: Future I 'werden … vergessen', V2 order

pronouns.english: Although he doesn't admit it himself, he's happy about every call from her
pronouns.hint: Obwohl-clause + main clause (V2). 'sich freuen ÜBER + Akk' = pleased about.

pronouns.english: There is nothing I wouldn't do for you, if you asked me to
pronouns.grammar:
Multi-layered: 'Es gibt nichts, was …' + Konjunktiv II 'würde tun', + 'wenn' clause with another Konjunktiv II 'bitten würdest'.
'darum' = 'for that' (replaces preposition + thing).

pronouns.english: The longer I talk with him, the better I understand his viewpoint
pronouns.grammar:
JE … DESTO construction (the more … the more):
• 'je + comparative' → verb to END of subordinate clause
• 'desto + comparative' → V2 in main clause

pronouns.english: Whoever told you that hasn't informed you correctly
pronouns.hint: 'Wer auch immer' = whoever. Subordinate verb at end, then 'hat' (V2) in main clause.

pronouns.english: We both know that you could concentrate even better if you really wanted to
pronouns.grammar:
'dass'-clause + reflexive 'sich konzentrieren' + Konjunktiv II 'könntest' & 'wolltest'.
Nested subordinate clauses — both verbs at the end of their own clauses.

tech.hint: 'der' = masculine 'the'
WLAN = Wireless LAN (the German word for WiFi)
In Germany, mobile phone = 'das Handy' (false-friend English word!)
'den' = Akkusativ masculine

tech.grammar:
SEPARABLE VERB: 'herunterladen'.
In present tense, the prefix moves to the end:
• herunterladen → Ich lade … herunter
• Other examples: aufstehen → Ich stehe … auf

tech.hint: 'klicken auf' + Akkusativ (action-based: 'den')
Separable verb 'ausschalten' → 'schalte … aus'
'im' = in + dem (Dativ, neuter)
Perfekt: hast + Partizip II at the end

tech.english: I need to install the software
tech.hint: Modal verb 'muss' → main verb at the end
Time expression 'jeden Tag' uses Akkusativ

tech.grammar:
Preposition 'mit' takes DATIV.
Adjective ending agrees: 'künstlich' + Dativ feminine → 'künstlicher'

tech.hint: Reflexive separable verb: 'sich einloggen'

tech.english: Don't forget to save your work
tech.grammar:
INFINITIVE WITH 'ZU':
• Used after verbs like 'vergessen', 'versuchen', 'anfangen'
• Pattern: verb + (object) + zu + Infinitiv (at end)

tech.english: Please send me the file by email
tech.hint: 'mir' = Dativ recipient, 'die Datei' = Akkusativ direct object
Separable verb 'abstürzen' → 'stürzt … ab'

tech.english: The database stores all user data
tech.hint: Compound nouns: Daten + Bank, Benutzer + Daten

tech.english: I have to update the system tomorrow
tech.grammar:
Word order: Time (morgen) → Direct object (das System) → Verb at end.
TeKaMoLo rule: Time, Kausal, Modal, Lokal.

FUTURE I tense:
• werden + Infinitiv (at the end)
• 'wird … verändern' = 'will change'
Note: Germans often use present tense with 'morgen', 'bald' instead of future.

tech.english: Although technology advances, some problems remain
tech.grammar:
OBWOHL-clause (although):
• 'Obwohl' is a subordinator → verb goes to END
• Then main clause: V2 → 'bleiben' starts
• 'bestehen' = 'to persist/remain in existence'

tech.hint: 'neues' = neuter adjective ending with 'mein'

tech.english: I need a new charging cable
tech.hint: Compound noun: Lade (charge) + Kabel (cable). Neuter → 'neues'.

tech.english: Press the button on the right
tech.hint: Informal imperative: 'Drück' (from drücken)

tech.english: My father uses the internet daily

tech.hint: Separable verb 'neu starten' or 'neustarten' → 'starte … neu'

tech.english: We have a video call today
tech.hint: Word order: subject + verb + time + Akk-object

tech.english: I sent the photo to my phone
tech.hint: Perfekt: haben + geschickt at end. 'auf' (motion to) + Akk.

tech.english: Which browser is better in your opinion?
tech.grammar:
Fixed expression 'deiner Meinung nach' = in your opinion.
Literally: 'after your opinion'. 'nach' here is a POSTPOSITION (rare!).

tech.english: The keyboard is set to German
tech.hint: Compound noun: Tast (key) + atur. 'auf Deutsch eingestellt' = set to German.

tech.english: You should regularly back up your data to avoid losses
tech.grammar:
MODAL 'sollten' + 'um … zu + Infinitiv' (purpose clause).
'um zu' = 'in order to' (only when same subject!)

tech.english: My smartphone automatically saves all photos in the cloud
Before you install the program, please read the instructions carefully
tech.grammar:
'bevor'-clause = subordinate → verb at end.
Main clause: imperative 'lies' (from lesen).

tech.english: Most modern websites also work well on the phone
tech.hint: 'auf dem Handy' = on the phone (Dat. for location)

tech.english: I spend too much time in front of the screen and need a break
tech.hint: 'vor + Dat' (location). Two clauses joined by 'und' (coordinating, no verb movement).

tech.english: Without a good password anyone can access your data
tech.grammar:
'ohne' + Akk → 'ein gutes' (neut. Akk).
'auf etwas zugreifen' = to access something (preposition + Akk).

tech.english: My brother repairs old computers and sells them online
tech.hint: Coordinating 'und' — both verbs in V2 position.

tech.english: During the update, you must not turn off the computer
tech.grammar:
'während' + GENITIV (formal): 'des Updates'.
'dürfen + nicht' = must NOT (prohibition, ≠ müssen + nicht).

tech.english: Have you tried simply restarting the device?
tech.grammar:
'versuchen … zu + Infinitiv' construction.
'versuchen' takes 'zu + Infinitiv' to express 'try to do'.

tech.english: The app I recommended to you is really practical
tech.hint: Relative clause 'die ich dir empfohlen habe' → verb 'habe' at end.

tech.english: Although AI can automate many tasks, we still need human judgment
tech.grammar:
Obwohl-clause: modal 'kann' + infinitive 'automatisieren' both at end (modal final).
Main clause: V2 'brauchen'.
Neuter Akk adjective: 'menschliches Urteilsvermögen' (no article).

tech.english: If I had the chance, I'd like to learn more about cybersecurity because the topic is becoming more important
tech.grammar:
Triple-clause sentence: wenn (Konj II) + main (würde + Inf) + weil-clause.
'immer + comparative' = 'more and more X'.

tech.english: It's important that children learn how to use technology responsibly and critically
tech.hint: 'Es ist wichtig, dass …' (impersonal) + 'wie man …' (indirect question).

tech.english: Digitalization changes not only our work, but also how we communicate
tech.grammar:
'nicht nur … sondern auch' = not only … but also.
'sondern' is coordinating (no verb movement after it).
'die Art, wie …' = the way how.

tech.english: Should the system crash, please contact our technical support directly
tech.grammar:
INVERTED conditional (formal): 'Sollte X …' = 'If X should …' (no 'wenn' needed).
'sich wenden AN + Akk' = to turn to / contact.

tech.english: The more data we share online, the harder it becomes to protect our privacy
tech.grammar:
Je … desto: 'je mehr Daten' (subordinate, verb 'teilen' at end), 'desto schwieriger' (main, V2 'wird').
'es … zu + Inf' = it (subject) … to do.

shopping.english: I need bread
What does this cost?
I would like a coffee
shopping.grammar:
'möchte' = polite 'would like'.
Followed by Akkusativ:
• einen Kaffee (masc) / eine Tasse (fem) / ein Wasser (neut)

shopping.english: I want to buy milk
That is cheap
That is expensive
Where is the cashier?
I'll take an apple
Do you have eggs?
I need a bag
I am buying a new shirt
shopping.grammar:
Adjective ending with 'ein' (indefinite article):
• masc: einen neuEN Pullover
• fem: eine neuE Hose
• neut: ein neuES Hemd
• plural: neuE Schuhe

shopping.english: How much do the apples cost?
I am paying with a card
Do you have this in size 40?
I am looking for a present
Can you help me?
I'll take three of those
That is too expensive for me
shopping.hint: 'mir' = Dativ ('for me' opinion). Pattern: [Dativ] + zu + adjective.

shopping.english: Is there a discount?
shopping.grammar:
'Es gibt' = 'there is/are'. Always:
• 'es' as subject (fixed)
• AKKUSATIV object → 'einen Rabatt'

shopping.english: Where do I find books?
These shoes are too small
A receipt, please
Can I try this on?
shopping.grammar:
SEPARABLE VERB 'anprobieren' stays together at the end with a modal verb.
With no modal: 'Ich probiere das an.'

shopping.english: The dress is too expensive
I would like to return this
Do you have a smaller size?
shopping.grammar:
Comparative + adjective ending:
• klein → kleiner (smaller)
• kleiner + ein/eine ending → kleinerE Größe

shopping.english: I'll take the shirt that's in the window
shopping.grammar:
RELATIVE CLAUSE:
• 'das Hemd, das …' (the shirt that …)
• Relative pronoun 'das' matches the noun's gender (das Hemd)
• Verb 'ist' goes to the END of the relative clause

shopping.english: The pants don't fit
shopping.hint: 'die Hose' is singular feminine in German (English 'pants' is plural)

shopping.english: Can I speak with the manager?
I need an invoice for the company
Do you also deliver to my home?
This item was on sale
If it doesn't fit, I'll return it
shopping.grammar:
WENN-clause (conditional):
• 'wenn' pushes verb to the end → 'wenn es nicht passt'
• Then the main clause starts with the verb → 'gebe ich' (V2 rule)

shopping.english: The receipt that you gave me is wrong
shopping.hint: Relative clause 'den Sie mir gegeben haben' — verb goes to the end

shopping.english: I would like this in a different color
shopping.grammar:
KONJUNKTIV II 'hätte' = polite 'would have/like'.
'in einer anderen Farbe':
• 'in' + Dativ feminine → 'einer'
• Adjective ending: '-en' after 'einer' → 'anderen'

shopping.english: If the item arrives damaged, complain to the seller
shopping.grammar:
FALLS = 'in case / if' (more formal than 'wenn').
'sich beschweren' = reflexive ('to complain').
'beim' = bei + dem (Dativ masc).

shopping.english: One bread, please
Where are the tomatoes?
I have no money
That is all
A bottle of water, please
How much does the banana cost?
Can you show me the size?
shopping.hint: 'zeigen' takes 2 objects: Dativ (recipient) + Akk (thing). 'zeigen' goes to end with modal.

shopping.english: I pay in cash
shopping.hint: Idiom 'in bar' (in cash) — fixed expression, no article

shopping.english: Do you have a bag for me?
The fruit is fresh today
We're looking for a gift for our daughter
Please write down the price
Which color do you like better, blue or red?
shopping.grammar:
'gefallen' takes DATIV (the experiencer):
• Mir gefällt das = I like that (lit: 'it pleases me')
• Welche Farbe gefällt dir = Which color do you like

shopping.english: We mostly shop on the weekend
If you pay with the card, you don't need a PIN for small amounts
shopping.hint: Wenn-clause + main clause. 'mit + Dat'. Adjective endings: 'kleine Beträge' (Akk pl.).

shopping.english: I'd like to have the gift wrapped, is that possible?
shopping.grammar:
CAUSATIVE 'lassen': 'etwas machen lassen' = to have something done.
'einpacken lassen' = to have wrapped (someone else does it for you).

shopping.english: Can you tell me when the next delivery is coming?
shopping.hint: Indirect question with 'wann' — verb 'kommt' at end of subordinate.

shopping.english: These pants don't fit me, I need a size larger
shopping.hint: 'passen' + Dat (the wearer). 'eine Nummer größer' = idiom 'one size larger'.

shopping.english: In summer there's always a big clearance sale in our city
We have a special offer on all organic products today
Before I buy something, I always compare prices online
shopping.hint: Bevor-clause (verb at end) + main clause V2.

shopping.english: My husband doesn't like this shirt, so I'm exchanging it
shopping.grammar:
'deswegen' = therefore (causal connector).
Separable 'umtauschen' → 'tausche … um'.
Following 'deswegen': V2 in main clause.

shopping.english: Food prices have risen sharply in recent months
shopping.grammar:
PERFEKT with 'sein' (motion/state change verbs):
• steigen (rise) → ist gestiegen
• Other 'sein'-verbs: fahren, kommen, werden, sterben

shopping.english: These shoes look nice, but they are unfortunately too expensive
Had I known it was cheaper in the other store, I wouldn't have bought here
shopping.grammar:
KONJUNKTIV II in past (counterfactual):
• 'hätte' + Partizip II
• Both clauses use Konjunktiv II for unrealized condition + result.

shopping.english: It's amazing how much shopping habits have changed in the last ten years
shopping.hint: 'wie sehr' = how much. Reflexive Perfekt: 'sich … verändert haben'.

shopping.english: Although online shopping is convenient, I prefer to try on clothes in the store
shopping.grammar:
'vorziehen' (separable) = to prefer.
'es … zu + Inf' (anticipatory pronoun 'es'): 'ziehe es vor … anzuprobieren'.
'an-zu-probieren' = separable verb with infinitive zu inserted in middle!

shopping.english: The saleswoman promised me the goods would be delivered by Friday
shopping.grammar:
PASSIV ('werden' + Partizip II): 'geliefert wird' = will be delivered.
'versprechen' takes Dativ (recipient of promise).

shopping.english: It's not worth buying cheap devices that break after a short time
Should you be dissatisfied with your purchase, you can return it within 30 days
shopping.hint: Falls + Konjunktiv II 'sollten'. 'mit + Dat'. 'innerhalb von + Dat'.

time-numbers.english: It is one o'clock
I have two children
Today is Monday
It is three o'clock
I am ten minutes late
Tomorrow is Saturday
It is noon
I have one brother
She has three dogs
Today is my birthday
It is half past three
time-numbers.grammar:
⚠️ TRICKY: 'halb vier' = 3:30 (literally 'half FOUR' = halfway to four).
Germans think of time as 'progress toward the next hour':
• 3:30 → halb vier
• 7:30 → halb acht

time-numbers.english: I work eight hours a day
My birthday is in May
The meeting starts at nine
time-numbers.grammar:
Time prepositions:
• um + clock time: um 9 Uhr
• am + day: am Montag
• im + month/season: im Mai, im Sommer
• in + year: in 2026 OR just '2026' alone

time-numbers.english: I am twenty-five years old
time-numbers.grammar:
Numbers 21-99: ones FIRST, then tens, joined by 'und':
• 21 = ein-und-zwanzig (one-and-twenty)
• 25 = fünf-und-zwanzig
• 47 = sieben-und-vierzig

time-numbers.english: She comes on Friday
In winter it is cold
We eat at seven
The break lasts twenty minutes
It is a quarter past eight
time-numbers.hint: 8:15 = Viertel nach acht. 8:45 = Viertel vor neun.

time-numbers.english: I jog every morning
We have time on the weekend
I have been living here for three years
time-numbers.grammar:
'seit' (since/for) takes DATIV.
Note: German uses PRESENT tense for ongoing actions where English uses present perfect.
• 'Ich wohne seit 3 Jahren' = 'I have been living for 3 years'

time-numbers.english: The store opens at quarter past eight
I will call you tomorrow morning
We meet every Friday
time-numbers.grammar:
REFLEXIVE VERB 'sich treffen' (to meet each other).
Reflexive pronouns: mich/dich/sich/uns/euch/sich.
'jeden Freitag' = Akkusativ time expression.

time-numbers.english: Two weeks ago I was sick
time-numbers.grammar:
'vor' + Dativ for past time:
• vor zwei Wochen = two weeks ago
• vor drei Tagen = three days ago
Note V2: 'war' is the verb, 'ich' goes AFTER it because time came first.

time-numbers.english: In three days I am going to Berlin
time-numbers.hint: 'in' + Dativ for future time. V2 word order again.

time-numbers.english: I am staying for another hour
We waited for over an hour
On the weekend I sleep late
time-numbers.hint: German 'lange schlafen' literally = 'sleep long' (= sleep in)

time-numbers.english: She always comes on time
The deadline is on the fifteenth of June
time-numbers.grammar:
ORDINAL NUMBERS take adjective endings.
Datum: am [Ordinal-en] [Monat]
• 1st = ersten, 3rd = dritten, 7th = siebten, 15th = fünfzehnten
General rule: number + 'ten' (1-19) or 'sten' (20+)

time-numbers.english: In a few minutes the train arrives
time-numbers.hint: Time first → verb second (V2 rule): kommt der Zug

time-numbers.english: Within two weeks we must decide
time-numbers.grammar:
'innerhalb' (within) — formal preposition.
With 'von' → Dativ: 'von zwei Wochen'.
Or formal: 'innerhalb zweier Wochen' (Genitiv).

time-numbers.english: During summer vacation I work as an intern
time-numbers.grammar:
'während' (during) → GENITIV (formally) or DATIV (colloquially).
• der Sommerferien (Genitiv plural)
• 'als' = 'as (a role)' — no article needed: 'als Praktikant'

time-numbers.english: It is seven o'clock
Today is Wednesday
I have four friends
She is sixteen years old
Tomorrow is Sunday
It is evening
School starts at 8 o'clock
In spring the weather is nice
My father will turn fifty next week
time-numbers.hint: 'werden' (become) for age changes. 'nächste Woche' = Akk time expression.

time-numbers.english: This evening I'm going to the cinema
time-numbers.hint: 'ins' = in + das (Akk — motion to). Time first → V2.

time-numbers.english: We have a meeting on Tuesday
It is twenty to nine
She returns from vacation in August
We see each other after lunch
I've been waiting for two hours for the next bus to the city center
time-numbers.hint: 'seit + Dat' for duration. PRESENT tense (German uses present for ongoing actions). 'warten auf + Akk'.

time-numbers.english: My father has been working at the same company for almost thirty years
Three days ago I met my old friend at the supermarket
We always meet on the first Saturday of the month for coffee
time-numbers.grammar:
ORDINAL number: 'ersten' (first, Dat. masc.).
'zum' = zu + dem (purpose 'for coffee').
Reciprocal 'uns'.

time-numbers.english: The conference starts at 10 and ends around 4 in the afternoon
time-numbers.hint: 'gegen + Akk' for approximate time. Two clauses with 'und'.

time-numbers.english: My son entered the tenth grade last year
Nowadays many people go to bed later than before
In the next weeks I will have much less free time
time-numbers.grammar:
FUTURE I: werden + Infinitiv (haben).
'in + Dat' for future periods.
'weniger' = less (comparative of wenig).

time-numbers.english: During lunch break I usually eat something quick
time-numbers.hint: 'während + Gen'. 'etwas Schnelles' = something fast (neut. capital S).

time-numbers.english: Ten years ago no one would have believed that was possible
time-numbers.grammar:
KONJUNKTIV II (counterfactual past):
• 'hätte … geglaubt' (would have believed)
• 'wäre' (would be) — both Konj II forms
• 'dass'-clause: verb 'wäre' at end

time-numbers.english: Within the next months we must decide whether to move to a larger apartment or not
time-numbers.grammar:
'innerhalb + Gen' (formal). 'ob … oder nicht' = whether or not.
Mixed structure: modal main + 'ob'-clause + 'oder nicht' tag.

time-numbers.english: The older I get, the faster time seems to pass, which I never understood as a child
time-numbers.grammar:
Multi-construction: 'je … desto' + 'scheinen + zu + Inf' + relative 'was'.
'scheinen zu + Inf' = to seem to do.

time-numbers.english: It's been twenty years since I first traveled to Germany
time-numbers.hint: Fixed expression 'X Jahre her, dass …' = it's been X years since. 'zum ersten Mal' = for the first time.

time-numbers.english: If the delivery doesn't arrive by Thursday at the latest, we'll have to complain to you
time-numbers.grammar:
'falls' (in case) + Future I 'werden müssen' (modal in future).
'sich beschweren BEI + Dat' = to complain to someone.

time-numbers.english: During my studies I learned to organize my time much more efficiently than before
time-numbers.hint: Perfekt + 'zu + Inf' (lernen takes zu). Comparative 'effizienter als'.

time-numbers.english: Although I tried to be on time, traffic delayed me by more than half an hour
time-numbers.grammar:
Obwohl + Perfekt + 'zu + Inf' embedded.
Main clause: 'hat … aufgehalten' (separable 'aufhalten' in Perfekt).
'um + Akk' for difference quantity ('by half an hour').

health-body.grammar:
'weh tun' = to hurt. Separable: 'tut … weh'
Alternative with Dativ:
• Mir tut der Kopf weh (lit: 'to me hurts the head')

Pain compound nouns with 'Schmerzen' (pain):
• Kopfschmerzen (head), Bauchschmerzen (stomach), Halsschmerzen (throat), Rückenschmerzen (back), Zahnschmerzen (tooth)
Always used in PLURAL.

REFLEXIVE VERB 'sich fühlen' (to feel):
• ich fühle MICH, du fühlst DICH, er fühlt SICH
• wir fühlen UNS, ihr fühlt EUCH, sie fühlen SICH

health-body.english: I have to go to the doctor
health-body.hint: 'zum' = zu + dem (Dativ). Note: no main verb because 'gehen' is implied/omitted
Informal imperative: verb stem only ('trink' from 'trinken')

health-body.english: I have an appointment with the doctor
You should drink a lot of water
health-body.grammar:
MODAL VERB 'sollten' (should — polite advice).
Pattern: Subject + sollte/solltest/sollten + … + main verb at end.

health-body.english: The medicine helps against the pain

health-body.grammar:
PERFEKT of reflexive verb 'sich erkälten':
• haben + reflexive pronoun + Partizip II
• Ich habe MICH erkältet
• Er hat SICH erkältet

REFLEXIVE PERFEKT: haben + sich + Partizip II.
'am Arm' = an + dem (Dativ) — location of the injury.
German uses Dativ for body parts in 'sich verletzen an + Dativ'.

health-body.english: I am going to the hospital tomorrow

health-body.hint: Perfekt: hat + mir (Dativ) + Tabletten (Akk) + Partizip II at end

health-body.english: Without glasses I cannot see anything
health-body.hint: 'ohne' + Akkusativ — but no article, so 'Brille' stays unchanged

health-body.grammar:
Idiomatic DATIV expression:
• Mir ist schlecht = I feel sick/nauseous
• Mir ist kalt = I am cold
• Mir ist langweilig = I am bored
No verb 'sein' for the feeler — feeler is in Dativ.

health-body.english: If you exercise regularly, you stay healthy
health-body.hint: Wenn-clause: verb at end of subordinate, then V2 in main

health-body.english: The pain has been getting worse for days
health-body.grammar:
Comparative 'schlimmer' (worse).
'werden' = to become.
'seit' + Dativ for duration.

health-body.english: It is important to take care of one's health
health-body.grammar:
'dass'-CLAUSE (that …):
• Subordinator → verb to END
• 'man' = impersonal 'one/you'
• 'achten AUF + Akkusativ' = 'to pay attention to'

health-body.english: Had I gone to the doctor earlier, I would already be healthy
health-body.grammar:
KONJUNKTIV II PAST (counterfactual):
• Wäre/hätte + Partizip II (at the end of subordinate)
• Main clause: V2 — 'wäre ich' starts after the subordinate clause
• Expresses regret about the past.

health-body.english: Please breathe in deeply and out slowly
health-body.hint: Two separable verbs: 'einatmen' (breathe in) + 'ausatmen' (breathe out). Imperative.

health-body.english: I was at the dentist yesterday
health-body.hint: 'beim' = bei + dem (location at). Compound noun: Zahn + Arzt.

health-body.english: My son has a sore throat and fever
You must take your tablets daily
health-body.hint: Modal 'musst' → main verb at end. 'täglich' = daily.

health-body.english: We go to the sports doctor on Friday
She has blue eyes and brown hair
health-body.hint: Plural adjective endings: 'blaue' (Akk plural without article)

health-body.english: My doctor advised me to eat more vegetables and fruit
health-body.grammar:
'jemandem raten + zu + Inf' = to advise someone to do.
'raten' takes Dativ ('mir').
The 'zu + Inf' clause follows after the comma.

health-body.english: If the pain doesn't go away, you'd best go to the hospital
She injured her ankle yesterday during sports
health-body.grammar:
REFLEXIVE Perfekt 'sich verletzen'.
Body part with Dativ + 'an + Dat':
• sich am Knöchel verletzen = to hurt one's ankle

health-body.english: I take two tablets every morning for my high blood pressure
health-body.hint: 'jeden Morgen' = Akk time. 'gegen + Akk' (against, for medical use).

health-body.english: My grandfather wasn't in the hospital his entire life
health-body.hint: Fixed: 'sein Leben lang' = his whole life (Akk + lang = duration).

health-body.english: We should all be examined regularly, even when we're healthy
health-body.grammar:
CAUSATIVE 'sich untersuchen lassen' = to have oneself examined.
Reflexive + lassen + Inf.
'auch wenn' = even when (subordinate, verb at end).

health-body.english: Without enough sleep I can't concentrate well during the day
My sister is a nurse and works in shifts
The doctor recommended I drink more water and less coffee
After the operation he has to rest for another two weeks
health-body.hint: 'nach + Dat'. Reflexive separable 'sich ausruhen' = to rest.

health-body.english: If you suffer from constant headaches, you should definitely see a specialist
health-body.grammar:
'leiden UNTER + Dat' = to suffer from.
'aufsuchen' = formal 'to consult/visit (a doctor)'.
Mixed clauses: wenn-clause + modal 'sollten' in main.

health-body.english: Although modern medicine has made great progress, there are still many incurable diseases
health-body.hint: Obwohl + Perfekt → main: 'es gibt' + Akk + 'ohne + Akk'.

health-body.english: My father completely changed his diet after the doctor said his cholesterol was too high
health-body.grammar:
Multi-clause: Perfekt main + nachdem (after) + dass-clause.
'nachdem' uses Plusquamperfekt usually, but Perfekt is also OK here.

health-body.english: Had I started exercising regularly earlier, my back muscles would be much stronger today
health-body.grammar:
KONJUNKTIV II past (counterfactual): inverted 'Hätte ich' = if I had.
Main clause Konj II 'wäre … stärker'.
'Sport treiben' = to do sports (idiom).

health-body.english: It's important to consult a doctor at the first signs of illness, instead of waiting
health-body.grammar:
'Es ist wichtig, zu + Inf'.
'statt + zu + Inf' = instead of doing.
'bei + Dat' here = at/upon.

health-body.english: During my hospital stay the nurses cared for me around the clock
health-body.grammar:
'während + Gen': 'meines Krankenhausaufenthalts' (long compound noun!).
'rund um die Uhr' = around the clock (idiom).
Perfekt: 'haben … betreut'.
